title: Getting Started with soilgraph
text: soilgraph provides tools structuring soil profile descriptions, exporting JSON, generating profile visualizations. typical workflow : Prepare two-column field description table (Depth + description). Parse descriptions structured soil profile. Visualize profile. Export .soil.json format archival exchange.
code: library(soilgraph)

title: Getting Started with soilgraph
text: Field notes represented data frame Depth column containing depth ranges (e.g. "0-18 cm") description column natural language descriptions:
code: field_notes <- data.frame(   Depth = c("0-18 cm", "18-52 cm", "52-95 cm"),   description = c(     "Ap dark brown silt loam moist clear smooth few subangular weak fragments with granular structure",     "Bt1 reddish brown clay loam slightly moist gradual wavy common rounded moderate fragments with clay films",     "Bt2 brown clay dry diffuse irregular many subrounded strong fragments with strong blocky structure"   ) ) field_notes #>      Depth #> 1  0-18 cm #> 2 18-52 cm #> 3 52-95 cm #>                                                                                                 description #> 1          Ap dark brown silt loam moist clear smooth few subangular weak fragments with granular structure #> 2 Bt1 reddish brown clay loam slightly moist gradual wavy common rounded moderate fragments with clay films #> 3        Bt2 brown clay dry diffuse irregular many subrounded strong fragments with strong blocky structure

title: Getting Started with soilgraph
text: derive_soil_horizons() extracts horizon properties (texture, color, moisture, boundary, coarse fragments) description text returns tidy table: parser recognizes two input modes: Depth ranges: "0-18 cm", "18-52 cm", "52-95 cm" Top depths : "0", "18", "52" — requires profile_bottom argument.
code: horizons <- derive_soil_horizons(field_notes) horizons #>      Depth #> 1  0-18 cm #> 2 18-52 cm #> 3 52-95 cm #>                                                                                                 description #> 1          Ap dark brown silt loam moist clear smooth few subangular weak fragments with granular structure #> 2 Bt1 reddish brown clay loam slightly moist gradual wavy common rounded moderate fragments with clay films #> 3        Bt2 brown clay dry diffuse irregular many subrounded strong fragments with strong blocky structure #>   Horizon Top Bottom   Texture       Moisture   Color BoundaryGrade #> 1      Ap   0     18 silt loam          moist #5C4033         clear #> 2     Bt1  18     52 clay loam slightly moist #7B3F2A       gradual #> 3     Bt2  52     95      clay            dry #8B5A2B       diffuse #>   BoundaryShape CoarseAbundance CoarseShape CoarseGrade CoarseType CoarseSize #> 1        smooth             few  subangular        weak       <NA>       <NA> #> 2          wavy          common     rounded    moderate       <NA>       <NA> #> 3     irregular            many  subrounded      strong       <NA>       <NA> #>   CoarseColor CoarsePercent                             Notes #> 1        <NA>            NA fragments with granular structure #> 2        <NA>            NA         fragments with clay films #> 3        <NA>            NA   fragments with blocky structure top_depth_notes <- data.frame(   Depth = c("0", "18", "52"),   description = c(     "Ap dark brown silt loam moist clear smooth",     "Bt1 reddish brown clay loam gradual wavy",     "Bt2 brown clay dry diffuse irregular"   ) )  horizons_top <- derive_soil_horizons(top_depth_notes, profile_bottom = 95) horizons_top[, c("Horizon", "Top", "Bottom", "Texture", "Color")] #>   Horizon Top Bottom   Texture   Color #> 1      Ap   0     18 silt loam #5C4033 #> 2     Bt1  18     52 clay loam #7B3F2A #> 3     Bt2  52     95      clay #8B5A2B

title: Getting Started with soilgraph
text: soil_profile_from_table() converts field notes table soil_profile object, primary data structure used plotting export functions: can also build profiles manually individual horizons: Use validate_soil_profile() check depth ordering overlap constraints:
code: profile <- soil_profile_from_table(   field_notes,   site_id = "pedon-001",   classification = list(     system = "Soil Taxonomy",     taxon  = "Typic Hapludalf"   ),   metadata = list(country = "Chile", land_use = "cropland") ) h1 <- new_soil_horizon(0, 18, label = "Ap", color = "#5C4033",   texture = "silt loam", boundary_grade = "clear", boundary_shape = "smooth") h2 <- new_soil_horizon(18, 52, label = "Bt1", color = "#8A5A44",   texture = "clay loam", boundary_grade = "gradual", boundary_shape = "wavy") h3 <- new_soil_horizon(52, 95, label = "Bt2", color = "#A66A4C",   texture = "clay", boundary_grade = "diffuse", boundary_shape = "irregular")  profile_manual <- new_soil_profile(   site_id = "pedon-001",   horizons = list(h1, h2, h3),   classification = list(system = "Soil Taxonomy", taxon = "Typic Hapludalf") ) validate_soil_profile(profile_manual)

title: Getting Started with soilgraph
text: Basic soil profile visualization.
code: plot_soil_profile(profile)

title: Getting Started with soilgraph
text: Point-based markers encode fragment type (shape), size (point size), cementation grade (alpha): Profile coarse fragment point markers.
code: plot_soil_profile_fragments(profile, seed = 42)

title: Getting Started with soilgraph
text: advanced renderer uses polygon-based fragments irregular boundary lines: Advanced profile polygon fragments, boundaries, transition zones.
code: plot_soil_profile_advanced(profile, seed = 42) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for alpha is already present. #> Adding another scale for alpha, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Getting Started with soilgraph
text: quick visualization without creating objects manually: Plot directly field description table.
code: plot_soil_description(field_notes, site_id = "pedon-001")

title: Getting Started with soilgraph
text: .soil.json format stores profiles metadata standardized schema: Read back:
code: # Write to a temporary file tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".soil.json") write_soil_json(profile, tmp)  # Inspect the JSON structure cat(readLines(tmp, n = 15), sep = "\n") #> { #>   "schema_version": "0.1.0", #>   "site_id": "pedon-001", #>   "classification": { #>     "system": "Soil Taxonomy", #>     "taxon": "Typic Hapludalf" #>   }, #>   "metadata": { #>     "country": "Chile", #>     "land_use": "cropland" #>   }, #>   "horizons": [ #>     { #>       "top": 0, #>       "bottom": 18, profile_reloaded <- read_soil_json(tmp) profile_reloaded$site_id #> [1] "pedon-001" profile_reloaded$classification$taxon #> [1] "Typic Hapludalf" length(profile_reloaded$horizons) #> [1] 3

title: Getting Started with soilgraph
text: package ships example files inst/extdata/:
code: json_path <- system.file("extdata", "example.soil.json", package = "soilgraph") example_profile <- read_soil_json(json_path) plot_soil_profile(example_profile)

title: Getting Started with soilgraph
text: Learn advanced graphical engines vignette("graphical-engines"). Explore soil data model supported vocabulary vignette("soil-data-model"). Browse gallery rendering examples vignette("rendering-examples").

title: Graphical Engines: Advanced Visualization
text: soilgraph ships three graphical engines convert soil morphological properties visual attributes: nbsp; | Engine | Encodes | Visual output | |—|—|—| | Coarse Fragment Engine | type, size, abundance, cementation grade | Irregular polygons | | Horizon Boundary Engine | shape (topography), grade (distinctness) | Irregular boundary lines | | Visualization Utilities | color, palette, theme, encoding | Themes helpers | primary entry point plot_soil_profile_advanced(), combines three engines single call.
code: library(soilgraph)

title: Graphical Engines: Advanced Visualization
text: reuse 3-horizon profile throughout article:
code: profile <- new_soil_profile(   site_id = "Engine-Demo",   classification = list(system = "Soil Taxonomy", taxon = "Typic Hapludalf"),   horizons = list(     new_soil_horizon(       top = 0, bottom = 18, label = "Ap",       texture = "silt loam", color = "#5C4033",       boundary_grade = "clear", boundary_shape = "smooth",       coarse_type = "gravel", coarse_abundance = "few",       coarse_grade = "weak", coarse_size = "fine"     ),     new_soil_horizon(       top = 18, bottom = 52, label = "Bt1",       texture = "clay loam", color = "#8A5A44",       boundary_grade = "gradual", boundary_shape = "wavy",       coarse_type = "cobble", coarse_abundance = "common",       coarse_grade = "moderate", coarse_size = "medium"     ),     new_soil_horizon(       top = 52, bottom = 95, label = "Bt2",       texture = "clay", color = "#A66A4C",       boundary_grade = "diffuse", boundary_shape = "irregular",       coarse_type = "stone", coarse_abundance = "many",       coarse_grade = "strong", coarse_size = "coarse"     )   ) )

title: Graphical Engines: Advanced Visualization
text: Compare three rendering modes: Basic profile — colored rectangles labels. Point-based fragment markers. Advanced rendering polygon fragments, boundary lines, transition zones.
code: plot_soil_profile(profile) plot_soil_profile_fragments(profile, seed = 42) plot_soil_profile_advanced(profile, seed = 42) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for alpha is already present. #> Adding another scale for alpha, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Graphical Engines: Advanced Visualization
text: can enable disable engine independently: Polygon fragments — boundaries disabled. Boundaries transition zones — fragments disabled.
code: plot_soil_profile_advanced(   profile,   show_fragments = TRUE,   show_boundaries = FALSE,   show_transition_zones = FALSE,   seed = 42 ) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale. plot_soil_profile_advanced(   profile,   show_fragments = FALSE,   show_boundaries = TRUE,   show_transition_zones = TRUE,   seed = 42 )

title: Graphical Engines: Advanced Visualization
text: Six fragment types supported, distinct polygon shape: fragment types equal abundance.
code: fragment_types <- c("gravel", "cobble", "stone", "boulder", "channer", "flagstone")  type_profile <- new_soil_profile(   site_id = "Fragment-Types",   horizons = lapply(seq_along(fragment_types), function(i) {     new_soil_horizon(       top = (i - 1) * 15, bottom = i * 15,       label = toupper(fragment_types[i]),       color = "#8B7355",       coarse_type = fragment_types[i],       coarse_abundance = "common",       coarse_grade = "moderate",       coarse_size = "medium",       boundary_shape = "smooth",       boundary_grade = "clear"     )   }) )  plot_soil_profile_advanced(type_profile, show_boundaries = FALSE, seed = 42) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Graphical Engines: Advanced Visualization
text: abundance property controls number fragments rendered horizon. Five levels supported: Fragment abundance gradient.
code: abundances <- c("very few", "few", "common", "many", "abundant")  abundance_profile <- new_soil_profile(   site_id = "Abundance-Gradient",   horizons = lapply(seq_along(abundances), function(i) {     new_soil_horizon(       top = (i - 1) * 15, bottom = i * 15,       label = abundances[i],       color = "#8B7355",       coarse_type = "gravel",       coarse_abundance = abundances[i],       coarse_grade = "moderate",       coarse_size = "medium",       boundary_shape = "smooth",       boundary_grade = "clear"     )   }) )  plot_soil_profile_advanced(abundance_profile, show_boundaries = FALSE, seed = 42) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Graphical Engines: Advanced Visualization
text: grade property mapped alpha (transparency), representing cementation weak (translucent) strong (opaque): Fragment cementation grade gradient.
code: grades <- c("very weak", "weak", "moderate", "strong", "very strong")  grade_profile <- new_soil_profile(   site_id = "Grade-Showcase",   horizons = lapply(seq_along(grades), function(i) {     new_soil_horizon(       top = (i - 1) * 15, bottom = i * 15,       label = grades[i],       color = "#8B7355",       coarse_type = "gravel",       coarse_abundance = "common",       coarse_grade = grades[i],       coarse_size = "medium",       boundary_shape = "smooth",       boundary_grade = "clear"     )   }) )  plot_soil_profile_advanced(grade_profile, show_boundaries = FALSE, seed = 42) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Graphical Engines: Advanced Visualization
text: Size mapped polygon scale: Fragment size class comparison.
code: sizes <- c("very fine", "fine", "small", "medium", "coarse", "large", "very coarse")  size_profile <- new_soil_profile(   site_id = "Size-Showcase",   horizons = lapply(seq_along(sizes), function(i) {     new_soil_horizon(       top = (i - 1) * 12, bottom = i * 12,       label = sizes[i],       color = "#8B7355",       coarse_type = "gravel",       coarse_abundance = "common",       coarse_grade = "moderate",       coarse_size = sizes[i],       boundary_shape = "smooth",       boundary_grade = "clear"     )   }) )  plot_soil_profile_advanced(size_profile, show_boundaries = FALSE, seed = 42) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Graphical Engines: Advanced Visualization
text: Shape determines distortion pattern boundary line:

title: Graphical Engines: Advanced Visualization
text: Grade controls line thickness dash pattern: Boundary grade shape combinations.
code: boundary_profile <- new_soil_profile(   site_id = "Boundary-Types",   horizons = list(     new_soil_horizon(0, 15, label = "Abrupt / Smooth", color = "#A0826D",       boundary_grade = "abrupt", boundary_shape = "smooth"),     new_soil_horizon(15, 30, label = "Clear / Wavy", color = "#9B7D66",       boundary_grade = "clear", boundary_shape = "wavy"),     new_soil_horizon(30, 50, label = "Gradual / Irregular", color = "#96795F",       boundary_grade = "gradual", boundary_shape = "irregular"),     new_soil_horizon(50, 75, label = "Diffuse / Broken", color = "#917558",       boundary_grade = "diffuse", boundary_shape = "broken")   ) )  plot_soil_profile_advanced(   boundary_profile,   show_fragments = FALSE,   show_boundaries = TRUE,   show_transition_zones = TRUE,   seed = 42 )

title: Graphical Engines: Advanced Visualization
text: Gradual diffuse boundaries generate transparent transition zones blend adjacent horizon colors. Enable show_transition_zones = TRUE (default).

title: Graphical Engines: Advanced Visualization
text: random elements (fragment placement, polygon perturbation, boundary noise) seeded. seed value always produces plot:
code: p1 <- plot_soil_profile_advanced(profile, seed = 42) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for alpha is already present. #> Adding another scale for alpha, which will replace the existing scale. p2 <- plot_soil_profile_advanced(profile, seed = 42) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for alpha is already present. #> Adding another scale for alpha, which will replace the existing scale.  # Both plots are identical identical(   ggplot2::ggplot_build(p1)$data,   ggplot2::ggplot_build(p2)$data ) #> [1] TRUE

title: Graphical Engines: Advanced Visualization
text: custom visualizations can call engines directly. functions internal (exported), use ::: operator: data frames can used standard ggplot2 geoms fully customized visualizations.
code: # Prepare horizon data horizon_data <- soilgraph:::build_horizon_plot_data(profile) head(horizon_data[, c("label", "top", "bottom", "fill", "boundary_shape", "coarse_type")]) #>   label top bottom    fill boundary_shape coarse_type #> 1    Ap   0     18 #5C4033         smooth      gravel #> 2   Bt1  18     52 #8A5A44           wavy      cobble #> 3   Bt2  52     95 #A66A4C      irregular       stone  # Generate boundary paths boundary_paths <- soilgraph:::build_boundary_paths(horizon_data, seed = 42) head(boundary_paths) #>             x  y boundary_id boundary_shape boundary_grade #> 1 0.000000000 18          b1         smooth          clear #> 2 0.005025126 18          b1         smooth          clear #> 3 0.010050251 18          b1         smooth          clear #> 4 0.015075377 18          b1         smooth          clear #> 5 0.020100503 18          b1         smooth          clear #> 6 0.025125628 18          b1         smooth          clear  # Generate fragment polygons fragment_polys <- soilgraph:::build_fragment_polygons(profile, seed = 42) head(fragment_polys) #>           x        y fragment_id fragment_type grade alpha   color #> 1 0.8653283 12.47324       h1_f1        gravel  weak  0.45 #1B1B1B #> 2 0.8535221 13.80316       h1_f1        gravel  weak  0.45 #1B1B1B #> 3 0.8348028 13.14962       h1_f1        gravel  weak  0.45 #1B1B1B #> 4 0.8192196 12.06979       h1_f1        gravel  weak  0.45 #1B1B1B #> 5 0.8273405 10.86124       h1_f1        gravel  weak  0.45 #1B1B1B #> 6 0.8495730 10.70867       h1_f1        gravel  weak  0.45 #1B1B1B

title: Graphical Engines: Advanced Visualization
text: three graphical engines translate soil morphological properties consistent visual encodings: Fragments: type → shape, size → scale, abundance → count, grade → alpha Boundaries: shape → distortion pattern, grade → line weight & dash Utilities: color validation, palette generation, contrast detection, theming engines deterministic (seeded), composable (toggle independently), built ggplot2 layers.

title: Rendering Examples Gallery
text: 3-horizon profile increasing fragment content progressively less-distinct boundaries depth: Typic Hapludalf — silt loam clay increasing coarse fragments.
code: profile_1 <- new_soil_profile(   site_id = "Hapludalf-CL",   classification = list(system = "Soil Taxonomy", taxon = "Typic Hapludalf"),   horizons = list(     new_soil_horizon(0, 18, label = "Ap", color = "#5C4033",       texture = "silt loam", boundary_grade = "clear", boundary_shape = "smooth",       coarse_type = "gravel", coarse_abundance = "few",       coarse_grade = "weak", coarse_size = "fine"),     new_soil_horizon(18, 52, label = "Bt1", color = "#8A5A44",       texture = "clay loam", boundary_grade = "gradual", boundary_shape = "wavy",       coarse_type = "cobble", coarse_abundance = "common",       coarse_grade = "moderate", coarse_size = "medium"),     new_soil_horizon(52, 95, label = "Bt2", color = "#A66A4C",       texture = "clay", boundary_grade = "diffuse", boundary_shape = "irregular",       coarse_type = "stone", coarse_abundance = "many",       coarse_grade = "strong", coarse_size = "coarse")   ) )  plot_soil_profile_advanced(profile_1, seed = 42) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for alpha is already present. #> Adding another scale for alpha, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Rendering Examples Gallery
text: deep sandy soil fragments smooth boundaries — clean, minimal rendering: Deep sandy profile smooth boundaries.
code: profile_2 <- new_soil_profile(   site_id = "Sandy-Profile",   classification = list(system = "Soil Taxonomy", taxon = "Typic Quartzipsamment"),   horizons = list(     new_soil_horizon(0, 20, label = "A", color = "#C2A878",       texture = "sand", boundary_grade = "clear", boundary_shape = "smooth"),     new_soil_horizon(20, 55, label = "C1", color = "#D4BC8E",       texture = "sand", boundary_grade = "gradual", boundary_shape = "smooth"),     new_soil_horizon(55, 100, label = "C2", color = "#E0CCA0",       texture = "sand", boundary_grade = "diffuse", boundary_shape = "smooth"),     new_soil_horizon(100, 150, label = "C3", color = "#E8D8B0",       texture = "loamy sand", boundary_grade = "diffuse", boundary_shape = "smooth")   ) )  plot_soil_profile_advanced(   profile_2,   show_fragments = FALSE,   show_boundaries = TRUE,   show_transition_zones = TRUE,   seed = 42 )

title: Rendering Examples Gallery
text: Abundant boulders channers thin dark soil rock debris: Stony colluvial soil abundant coarse fragments.
code: profile_3 <- new_soil_profile(   site_id = "Colluvial-01",   classification = list(system = "Field description", taxon = NULL),   horizons = list(     new_soil_horizon(0, 12, label = "A", color = "#3D2B1F",       texture = "loam", boundary_grade = "abrupt", boundary_shape = "irregular",       coarse_type = "channer", coarse_abundance = "common",       coarse_grade = "strong", coarse_size = "small"),     new_soil_horizon(12, 45, label = "C", color = "#6B5A4E",       texture = "sandy loam", boundary_grade = "clear", boundary_shape = "broken",       coarse_type = "boulder", coarse_abundance = "abundant",       coarse_grade = "very strong", coarse_size = "very coarse")   ) )  plot_soil_profile_advanced(profile_3, seed = 42) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Rendering Examples Gallery
text: six fragment types equal abundance grade, side side: six coarse fragment types.
code: frag_types <- c("gravel", "cobble", "stone", "boulder", "channer", "flagstone")  profile_4 <- new_soil_profile(   site_id = "Fragment-Types",   horizons = lapply(seq_along(frag_types), function(i) {     new_soil_horizon(       top = (i - 1) * 15, bottom = i * 15,       label = frag_types[i],       color = "#8B7355",       coarse_type = frag_types[i],       coarse_abundance = "common",       coarse_grade = "moderate",       coarse_size = "medium",       boundary_shape = "smooth",       boundary_grade = "clear"     )   }) )  plot_soil_profile_advanced(profile_4, show_boundaries = FALSE, seed = 42) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Rendering Examples Gallery
text: Four grade × shape combinations transition zones: Boundary grade shape demonstration.
code:
profile_5 <- new_soil_profile(   site_id = "Boundary-Demo",   horizons = list(     new_soil_horizon(0, 15, label = "Abrupt
Smooth", color = "#A0826D",       boundary_grade = "abrupt", boundary_shape = "smooth"),     new_soil_horizon(15, 30, label = "Clear
Wavy", color = "#9B7D66",       boundary_grade = "clear", boundary_shape = "wavy"),     new_soil_horizon(30, 50, label = "Gradual
Irregular", color = "#96795F",       boundary_grade = "gradual", boundary_shape = "irregular"),     new_soil_horizon(50, 75, label = "Diffuse
Broken", color = "#917558",       boundary_grade = "diffuse", boundary_shape = "broken")   ) )  plot_soil_profile_advanced(   profile_5,   show_fragments = FALSE,   show_boundaries = TRUE,   show_transition_zones = TRUE,   seed = 42 )

title: Rendering Examples Gallery
text: abundant fragments identical horizons: Fragment abundance increasing depth.
code: abundances <- c("very few", "few", "common", "many", "abundant")  profile_6 <- new_soil_profile(   site_id = "Abundance-Gradient",   horizons = lapply(seq_along(abundances), function(i) {     new_soil_horizon(       top = (i - 1) * 15, bottom = i * 15,       label = abundances[i],       color = "#8B7355",       coarse_type = "gravel",       coarse_abundance = abundances[i],       coarse_grade = "moderate",       coarse_size = "medium",       boundary_shape = "smooth",       boundary_grade = "clear"     )   }) )  plot_soil_profile_advanced(profile_6, show_boundaries = FALSE, seed = 42) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.

what: 7. From field notes to advanced plot
title: Rendering Examples Gallery
text: End--end workflow — field notes parsed rendered advanced engines: Field description parsed rendered engines.
code: field_notes <- data.frame(   Depth = c("0-18 cm", "18-52 cm", "52-95 cm"),   description = c(     "Ap dark brown silt loam moist clear smooth few subangular weak fragments",     "Bt1 reddish brown clay loam slightly moist gradual wavy common rounded moderate fragments",     "Bt2 brown clay dry diffuse irregular many subrounded strong fragments"   ) )  profile_7 <- soil_profile_from_table(   field_notes,   site_id = "Field-to-Plot",   classification = list(system = "Soil Taxonomy", taxon = "Typic Hapludalf") )  plot_soil_profile_advanced(profile_7, seed = 42) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for alpha is already present. #> Adding another scale for alpha, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Rendering Examples Gallery
text: profile rendered three different seeds — structures differ properties preserved: Seed = 1 — first random layout. Seed = 42 — different fragment placement. Seed = 99 — yet another layout.
code: plot_soil_profile_advanced(profile_1, seed = 1) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for alpha is already present. #> Adding another scale for alpha, which will replace the existing scale. plot_soil_profile_advanced(profile_1, seed = 42) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for alpha is already present. #> Adding another scale for alpha, which will replace the existing scale. plot_soil_profile_advanced(profile_1, seed = 99) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for alpha is already present. #> Adding another scale for alpha, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Soil Description Data Model
text: soilgraph uses two-level data model: profiles contain horizons. level carries set properties. article documents supported fields, vocabularies, .soil.json storage format.
code: library(soilgraph)

title: Soil Description Data Model
text: soil_profile object stores site-level metadata alongside list horizons: nbsp; | Field | Type | Required | Description | |——-|——|———-|————-| | schema_version | string | auto | Schema version (currently "0.1.0") | | site_id | string | yes | Unique site identifier | | classification$system | string | yes | Classification system ("Soil Taxonomy", "WRB") | | classification$taxon | string | | Taxon name (e.g. "Typic Hapludalf") | | metadata | named list | | Arbitrary key-value pairs (country, land use, slope, etc.) |
code: profile <- new_soil_profile(   site_id = "pedon-042",   horizons = list(     new_soil_horizon(0, 25, label = "A"),     new_soil_horizon(25, 60, label = "Bw")   ),   classification = list(system = "WRB", taxon = "Haplic Cambisol"),   metadata = list(country = "Chile", slope_percent = 8, land_use = "forest") )  profile$site_id #> [1] "pedon-042" profile$classification #> $system #> [1] "WRB" #>  #> $taxon #> [1] "Haplic Cambisol" profile$metadata #> $country #> [1] "Chile" #>  #> $slope_percent #> [1] 8 #>  #> $land_use #> [1] "forest"

title: Soil Description Data Model
text: soil_horizon carries morphological properties. table lists supported fields:

title: Soil Description Data Model
code: h <- new_soil_horizon(   top = 0, bottom = 18,   label = "Ap",   texture = "silt loam",   color = "#5C4033",   moisture = "moist",   boundary_grade = "clear",   boundary_shape = "smooth",   coarse_abundance = "few",   coarse_shape = "subangular",   coarse_grade = "weak",   coarse_type = "gravel",   coarse_size = "fine",   coarse_color = "#1B1B1B",   coarse_percent = 5,   notes = "Granular structure, abundant roots" ) str(h, max.level = 1) #> List of 16 #>  $ top             : num 0 #>  $ bottom          : num 18 #>  $ label           : chr "Ap" #>  $ texture         : chr "silt loam" #>  $ color           : chr "#5C4033" #>  $ moisture        : chr "moist" #>  $ boundary_grade  : chr "clear" #>  $ boundary_shape  : chr "smooth" #>  $ coarse_abundance: chr "few" #>  $ coarse_shape    : chr "subangular" #>  $ coarse_grade    : chr "weak" #>  $ coarse_type     : chr "gravel" #>  $ coarse_size     : chr "fine" #>  $ coarse_color    : chr "#1B1B1B" #>  $ coarse_percent  : num 5 #>  $ notes           : chr "Granular structure, abundant roots" #>  - attr(*, "class")= chr [1:2] "soil_horizon" "list"

title: Soil Description Data Model
text: derive_soil_horizons() extracts structured data free-text field descriptions. parser uses keyword dictionaries recognize properties.

title: Soil Description Data Model
text: Depth ranges (e.g. "0-18 cm"): Top depths (requires profile_bottom):
code: notes_range <- data.frame(   Depth = c("0-18 cm", "18-52 cm", "52-95 cm"),   description = c(     "Ap dark brown silt loam moist clear smooth few subangular weak fragments",     "Bt1 reddish brown clay loam slightly moist gradual wavy common rounded moderate fragments",     "Bt2 brown clay dry diffuse irregular many subrounded strong fragments"   ) )  result <- derive_soil_horizons(notes_range) result[, c("Horizon", "Top", "Bottom", "Texture", "Color", "BoundaryGrade")] #>   Horizon Top Bottom   Texture   Color BoundaryGrade #> 1      Ap   0     18 silt loam #5C4033         clear #> 2     Bt1  18     52 clay loam #7B3F2A       gradual #> 3     Bt2  52     95      clay #8B5A2B       diffuse notes_top <- data.frame(   Depth = c("0", "18", "52"),   description = c(     "Ap dark brown silt loam moist",     "Bt1 reddish brown clay loam slightly moist",     "Bt2 brown clay dry"   ) )  result_top <- derive_soil_horizons(notes_top, profile_bottom = 95) result_top[, c("Horizon", "Top", "Bottom")] #>   Horizon Top Bottom #> 1      Ap   0     18 #> 2     Bt1  18     52 #> 3     Bt2  52     95

title: Soil Description Data Model
text: parser matches built-dictionaries. main categories: Texture classes: sandy clay, sandy clay loam, sandy loam, loamy sand, silt loam, silty clay, silty clay loam, clay loam, clay, loam, silt, sand. Moisture states: dry, slightly moist, moist, wet. Color names: dark brown, reddish brown, brown, yellowish brown, olive brown, gray, dark gray, dark reddish brown, red, yellowish red, dark yellowish brown, pale brown, light brownish gray, dark gray, dark brown, black, white. Boundary grades: abrupt, clear, gradual, diffuse. Boundary shapes: smooth, wavy, irregular, broken, discontinuous. Coarse fragment terms: abundance (, , common, many, abundant), shape (angular, subangular, subrounded, rounded, flat), grade (weak, weak, moderate, strong, strong).

title: Soil Description Data Model
text: Profiles serialized standardized JSON schema. format includes full profile structure horizons metadata.
code: profile_full <- soil_profile_from_table(   notes_range,   site_id = "pedon-001",   classification = list(system = "Soil Taxonomy", taxon = "Typic Hapludalf"),   metadata = list(country = "Chile") )  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".soil.json") write_soil_json(profile_full, tmp) cat(readLines(tmp), sep = "\n") #> { #>   "schema_version": "0.1.0", #>   "site_id": "pedon-001", #>   "classification": { #>     "system": "Soil Taxonomy", #>     "taxon": "Typic Hapludalf" #>   }, #>   "metadata": { #>     "country": "Chile" #>   }, #>   "horizons": [ #>     { #>       "top": 0, #>       "bottom": 18, #>       "label": "Ap", #>       "texture": "silt loam", #>       "color": "#5C4033", #>       "moisture": "moist", #>       "boundary_grade": "clear", #>       "boundary_shape": "smooth", #>       "coarse_abundance": "few", #>       "coarse_shape": "subangular", #>       "coarse_grade": "weak", #>       "coarse_type": null, #>       "coarse_size": null, #>       "coarse_color": null, #>       "coarse_percent": null, #>       "notes": "fragments" #>     }, #>     { #>       "top": 18, #>       "bottom": 52, #>       "label": "Bt1", #>       "texture": "clay loam", #>       "color": "#7B3F2A", #>       "moisture": "slightly moist", #>       "boundary_grade": "gradual", #>       "boundary_shape": "wavy", #>       "coarse_abundance": "common", #>       "coarse_shape": "rounded", #>       "coarse_grade": "moderate", #>       "coarse_type": null, #>       "coarse_size": null, #>       "coarse_color": null, #>       "coarse_percent": null, #>       "notes": "fragments" #>     }, #>     { #>       "top": 52, #>       "bottom": 95, #>       "label": "Bt2", #>       "texture": "clay", #>       "color": "#8B5A2B", #>       "moisture": "dry", #>       "boundary_grade": "diffuse", #>       "boundary_shape": "irregular", #>       "coarse_abundance": "many", #>       "coarse_shape": "subrounded", #>       "coarse_grade": "strong", #>       "coarse_type": null, #>       "coarse_size": null, #>       "coarse_color": null, #>       "coarse_percent": null, #>       "notes": "fragments" #>     } #>   ] #> }

title: Soil Description Data Model
text: bundled example file demonstrates complete schema:
code:
json_path <- system.file("extdata", "example.soil.json", package = "soilgraph") example <- read_soil_json(json_path)  cat("Site ID:", example$site_id, "
") #> Site ID: pedon-001 cat("System:", example$classification$system, "
") #> System: Soil Taxonomy cat("Taxon:", example$classification$taxon, "
") #> Taxon: Typic Hapludalf cat("Horizons:", length(example$horizons), "
") #> Horizons: 3 cat("Total depth:", example$horizons[[length(example$horizons)]]$bottom, "cm
") #> Total depth: 95 cm

title: Soil Description Data Model
text: Writing reading back preserves fields:
code: tmp2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".soil.json") write_soil_json(example, tmp2) reloaded <- read_soil_json(tmp2)  identical(example$site_id, reloaded$site_id) #> [1] TRUE identical(example$classification, reloaded$classification) #> [1] TRUE identical(length(example$horizons), length(reloaded$horizons)) #> [1] TRUE

title: Soil Description Data Model
text: validate_soil_profile() checks following constraints: object soil_profile class. horizons non-empty list soil_horizon objects. Top depths sorted ascending order (shallow → deep). horizon’s bottom > top. overlapping horizons.
code: # Valid profile passes silently validate_soil_profile(profile)  # Invalid profiles throw informative errors tryCatch(   validate_soil_profile(list(not = "a profile")),   error = function(e) message("Error: ", e$message) ) #> Error: `profile` must be a `soil_profile`.

title: Soil Description Data Model
text: key terms USDA Soil Survey Manual used throughout soilgraph: Horizon: layer soil approximately parallel soil surface, differing adjacent layers physical chemical properties. Boundary grade (distinctness): sharply one horizon transitions next — abrupt (< 2 cm), clear (2–5 cm), gradual (5–15 cm), diffuse (> 15 cm). Boundary shape (topography): spatial pattern boundary — smooth, wavy, irregular, broken, discontinuous. Coarse fragments: Rock mineral particles > 2 mm diameter. Classified size, shape, degree weathering (cementation grade). Texture class: relative proportion sand, silt, clay (e.g. “silt loam” = mostly silt sand clay).

what: Soil Profile Visualization from Field Descriptions
title: Soil Profile Visualization from Field Descriptions
text: usual way clasify soils pit description. sence, 2 main systems classifying soils: + Soil Taxonomy: Developed USDA United States America, widelly adopted Americas, Asia, Oceania Africa. + World Reference Base Soil Resources (WRB): FAO, used Europe. Focus soil physical medium. current package can: + Create vector database soil description systems way integrate systematic way. + Create standard store soil description (Initially just Soil Taxonomy system, WRB). .soil.json standarized fields based models. + Automate creation .soil.json based field data. Even handwriten. Usually every property note, can infered based general easy survey. + Graphical visualization soil profile based .soil.json object. Create visual interpretation based description criteria translated visual hints. + Coarse fragment engine can generate irregular elements based description, different shape, size, abundance, degree. + Horizon limit engine can generate irregular elements based description, different shape degree. + Tidy description creation.

title: Soil Profile Visualization from Field Descriptions

title: Soil Profile Visualization from Field Descriptions

title: Soil Profile Visualization from Field Descriptions
code: library(soilgraph)  field_notes <- data.frame(     Depth = c("0-18 cm", "18-52 cm", "52-95 cm"),     description = c(         "Ap dark brown silt loam moist clear smooth few subangular weak fragments with granular structure",         "Bt1 reddish brown clay loam slightly moist gradual wavy common rounded moderate fragments with clay films",         "Bt2 brown clay dry diffuse irregular many subrounded strong fragments with strong blocky structure"     ) )  derive_soil_horizons(field_notes)  profile <- soil_profile_from_table(field_notes, site_id = "pedon-001") plot_soil_description(field_notes, site_id = "pedon-001")  write_soil_json(profile, "example.soil.json")

title: Soil Profile Visualization from Field Descriptions
text: Horizon: inferred horizon label, generated fallback labels Top Bottom: parsed numeric depth boundaries centimeters Texture, Moisture, Color: rule-based fields extracted description BoundaryGrade BoundaryShape: horizon limit distinctness topography CoarseAbundance, CoarseShape, CoarseGrade: coarse fragment descriptors Notes: remaining free text structured tokens removed

what: Plot soil profile with advanced graphical engines — plot_soil_profile_advanced
title: Plot soil profile with advanced graphical engines — plot_soil_profile_advanced
text: Creates detailed soil profile visualization using custom graphical engines coarse fragments (polygons) horizon boundaries (irregular lines). function provides sophisticated rendering compared basic point-based visualization.

title: Plot soil profile with advanced graphical engines — plot_soil_profile_advanced
code: plot_soil_profile_advanced(   profile,   show_fragments = TRUE,   show_boundaries = TRUE,   show_transition_zones = TRUE,   seed = 1 )

title: Plot soil profile with advanced graphical engines — plot_soil_profile_advanced
text: profile soil_profile object. show_fragments Logical: whether render coarse fragments polygons. show_boundaries Logical: whether render boundary transitions. show_transition_zones Logical: whether show gradual/diffuse transition zones. seed Random seed deterministic rendering.

title: Plot soil profile with advanced graphical engines — plot_soil_profile_advanced
text: ggplot2 object.

title: soilgraph 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release. Structured soil objects (new_soil_horizon(), new_soil_profile()). Field description parser (derive_soil_horizons(), soil_profile_from_table()). Coarse fragment graphical engine irregular polygon rendering. Horizon boundary graphical engine shape grade distortion. Five plotting functions: plot_soil_profile(), plot_soil_profile_fragments(), plot_soil_profile_advanced(), plot_soil_description(), plot_soil_description_fragments(). .soil.json import/export via read_soil_json() write_soil_json(). Four vignettes: Getting Started, Graphical Engines, Soil Data Model, Rendering Examples Gallery.
